# afterly

afterly is an iOS app for people going through a breakup who are trying to maintain no contact with their ex.

## What it does

- **Urge button** — one tap opens a breathing exercise when the urge to text an ex peaks. The urge passes. The message never sends.
- **Unsent vault** — a private space to write everything you want to say. It saves. It never sends.
- **EMBER streak** — a visual orb that fills with light every day you stay no contact. Day 1 dark. Day 60 bright.

## Who it's for

People who know they shouldn't contact their ex but keep doing it anyway. The problem isn't willpower — it's the absence of something to do instead. afterly is that something.

## The science

Romantic rejection activates the same brain circuits as cocaine craving (Journal of Neurophysiology, 2010). No contact is neurologically difficult. afterly treats it as a recovery process, not a willpower test.

## Blog — topic coverage

The afterly blog covers breakup recovery science across five stages:

**Stage 1 — Why it hurts (Days 1–3)**
- Why a breakup hurts so much (neuroscience)
- Why you can't eat or sleep after a breakup
- Why you can't stop thinking about your ex
- Breakup grief vs. depression

**Stage 2 — Why no contact works (Days 4–7)**
- Why no contact works: what it does to your brain
- Why you should stop checking your ex's social media
- Why the urge to text gets worse before it gets better
- Why staying friends with your ex slows healing

**Stage 3 — Breaking the loop (Days 8–14)**
- Why songs and places make you miss your ex
- How to stop the urge to text your ex
- Why you miss your ex more at night
- Why getting over a breakup takes longer than you think

**Stage 4 — Rebuilding (Days 15–30)**
- Why a breakup makes you feel like you lost yourself
- Why rebounding doesn't fix self-worth
- How to start enjoying life again after a breakup
- Why small wins matter during breakup recovery

**Stage 5 — Long term (Days 30+)**
- Why breakup recovery is not linear
- I texted my ex — does that mean I have to start over?
- How to know you're getting over a breakup
- How to know if you're ready to date again

**Decision stage — Does no contact work?**
- Does the no contact rule actually work?
- What happens to your ex during no contact?
- Should I reach out to my ex?
- Do exes come back after no contact?

**Attachment & psychology**
- Anxious attachment and breakups
- Why you feel addicted to someone who is bad for you
- What is breadcrumbing and why it keeps you stuck
- Why your ex keeps coming back but never stays

**Specific painful moments**
- Why did they move on so fast?
- My ex is already dating someone new
- Why do I still love someone who hurt me?
- Why do I miss the relationship more than the person?

**Practical how-to**
- How to stop thinking about your ex at work
- How to handle mutual friends after a breakup
- What to do when you have to see your ex
- How to delete someone from your life without hating them

**No contact timelines**
- What to expect in the first 30 days of no contact
- No contact week by week: what actually happens

**Trauma bonding**
- What is a trauma bond and how do you know you have one?
- Why leaving a toxic relationship feels impossible
- How long does it take to break a trauma bond?

**Ghosting**
- What is ghosting and why does it hurt more than a normal breakup?
- Why did they ghost me? The psychology behind it
- How to get closure when you were ghosted
- Should you reach out after being ghosted?

**Situationships**
- What is a situationship and why are they so hard to leave?
- How to get over someone you were never officially with
- Why situationship grief feels invisible but is very real

## Download

Free to try. iPhone only. App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/afterly-no-contact-recovery/id6762640667
